content: The comparative performance variance between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and hand-designed curious agents can be explained by unaccounted differences in how each agent type encodes and retrieves episodic memory to direct intrinsic exploration. We hypothesize that current comparisons of curiosity-driven RL methods conflate exploration reward formulation with underlying agent memory architecture, leading to misattributed performance gaps.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
